questions.question: Who is considered the founder of behaviorism?
In classical conditioning, the unconditioned stimulus (UCS) is:
options: A stimulus that triggers a learned response
A stimulus that naturally triggers a response
A response to a learned stimulus

questions.question: In classical conditioning, the conditioned stimulus (CS) is:
options: A stimulus that naturally triggers a response
A previously neutral stimulus that triggers a learned response
A response to a natural stimulus

questions.question: In Pavlov's experiment, the food was the:
In Pavlov's experiment, the bell was the:
In Pavlov's experiment, salivation to the bell was the:
Acquisition in classical conditioning refers to:
options: The weakening of a conditioned response
The initial learning of the conditioned response
The extinction of a conditioned response
The spontaneous recovery of a response

questions.question: Extinction in classical conditioning occurs when:
options: The CS is presented repeatedly without the UCS
The UCS is presented repeatedly without the CS

questions.question: Spontaneous recovery refers to:
options: The permanent disappearance of a response
The reappearance of an extinct response after a rest period
The initial learning of a response

questions.question: Stimulus generalization occurs when:
options: A response is only triggered by the original CS
A response is triggered by stimuli similar to the CS

questions.question: Stimulus discrimination occurs when:
options: A response is triggered by all stimuli
A response is triggered only by the original CS

questions.question: The law of effect was proposed by:
The law of effect states that:
options: All behaviors are equally likely to be repeated
Behaviors followed by positive consequences are more likely to be repeated
Behaviors followed by negative consequences are more likely to be repeated

questions.question: Reinforcement is defined as:
options: Any consequence that has no effect on behavior

questions.question: Punishment is defined as:
options: Any consequence that has no effect on behavior

questions.question: Positive reinforcement involves:
options: Adding a pleasant stimulus to increase behavior
Removing a pleasant stimulus to increase behavior
Adding an unpleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Removing an unpleasant stimulus to increase behavior

questions.question: Negative reinforcement involves:
options: Adding a pleasant stimulus to increase behavior
Removing a pleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Adding an unpleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Removing an unpleasant stimulus to increase behavior

questions.question: Positive punishment involves:
options: Adding a pleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Adding an unpleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Removing a pleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Removing an unpleasant stimulus to decrease behavior

questions.question: Negative punishment involves:
options: Adding a pleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Adding an unpleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Removing a pleasant stimulus to decrease behavior
Removing an unpleasant stimulus to decrease behavior

questions.question: A reinforcer that satisfies a biological need is called:
A reinforcer that is learned through association is called:
Continuous reinforcement schedule means:
options: Reinforcement after a fixed number of responses

questions.question: Fixed-ratio schedule means:
options: Reinforcement after a fixed number of responses
Reinforcement after a variable number of responses
Reinforcement after a fixed time interval
Reinforcement after a variable time interval

questions.question: Variable-ratio schedule means:
options: Reinforcement after a fixed number of responses
Reinforcement after a variable number of responses
Reinforcement after a fixed time interval
Reinforcement after a variable time interval

questions.question: Fixed-interval schedule means:
options: Reinforcement after a fixed number of responses
Reinforcement after a variable number of responses
Reinforcement after a fixed time interval
Reinforcement after a variable time interval

questions.question: Variable-interval schedule means:
options: Reinforcement after a fixed number of responses
Reinforcement after a variable number of responses
Reinforcement after a fixed time interval
Reinforcement after a variable time interval

questions.question: Shaping is a technique used to:
options: Reinforce successive approximations of a target behavior

Escape learning involves:
options: Performing a behavior to avoid an unpleasant stimulus
Performing a behavior to end an unpleasant stimulus
Performing a behavior to receive a reward
Performing a behavior to avoid a reward

questions.question: Avoidance learning involves:
options: Performing a behavior to end an unpleasant stimulus
Performing a behavior to avoid an unpleasant stimulus before it occurs
Performing a behavior to receive a reward
Performing a behavior to avoid a reward

questions.question: Learned helplessness occurs when:
options: An organism learns that it can control its environment
An organism learns that it cannot control its environment
An organism learns new behaviors easily

questions.question: Albert Bandura's Bobo doll experiment demonstrated:
Latent learning is:
options: Learning that is not immediately demonstrated but is present

questions.question: A cognitive map is:
options: A physical map of the environment
A mental representation of the environment
A written description of the environment

Biofeedback is a technique that:
options: Uses operant conditioning to control bodily processes

Aversive conditioning involves:
options: Using unpleasant stimuli to change behavior

questions.question: Flooding is a behavior therapy technique that involves:
options: Gradual exposure to a feared stimulus
Intense exposure to a feared stimulus

questions.question: Systematic desensitization involves:
options: Sudden exposure to a feared stimulus
Gradual exposure to a feared stimulus with relaxation

questions.question: The Premack principle states that:
options: Low-frequency behaviors can reinforce high-frequency behaviors
High-frequency behaviors can reinforce low-frequency behaviors
Punishment is more effective than reinforcement

options: Only observable behavior should be studied

options: Replace bad habits with good ones

questions.question: In behavior modification, generalization is:
options: The application of learning to new situations
The restriction of learning to one situation

questions.question: Discrimination training involves:
options: Reinforcing behavior only in specific situations

options: Therapy, education, parenting, and animal training
